rules.rule: Components MUST be pure rendering functions without side effects or business logic.
Components MUST receive all data via props, never fetch data directly.
Components MUST use TypeScript for props definition.
Components MUST be in src/components/{domain}/ directory.
Component files MUST follow pattern {Domain}{Purpose}.tsx (e.g., PatientCard.tsx).
Components MUST export as named exports, not default exports.
Components MUST handle loading/error states via props, not internal state.
Components MUST NOT call services, brokers, or APIs directly.
Components MUST delegate all business logic to services via page hooks.
Components MUST NOT use useEffect for data fetching.
Components MUST use function component syntax, not class components.
Component props MUST be destructured in function signature.
Components SHOULD be under 200 lines; split if larger.
Components MUST have single responsibility.
Event handlers MUST be passed as props, not defined inline when complex.
Props type MUST be defined in models/components/{domain}/.
Optional props MUST use ? syntax.
Callback props MUST be optional unless always required.
Props MUST NOT include foundation models directly.
Components SHOULD use view models for data props.
